red_baseline.reason: T023 owns adapter-exit and compatible/incompatible runtime takeover contracts; any compatibility gate repair belongs to T024.
red_baseline.summary: 2/3 pass; the incompatible replacement alone still reports canWrite=true, isolating the production gap assigned to T024.

validation.summary: Adapter exit and compatible Codex-to-Claude takeover pass with one spawn/run; incompatible write capability remains the sole intentional RED at T024's boundary.

review.evidence: The suites use the real stdio MCP and CLI entrypoints, assert PID/run/port/state identity and one spawn receipt, and isolate the only production gap to compatibility propagation rather than introducing test-owned behavior.

obligation_evidence.evidence: Both public adapters observe one Kernel-owned run and state authority.
The tests require component identity, not package version alone, to decide compatibility.
Closing either MCP adapter leaves the managed process and shared state intact.
Real entrypoint lifecycle receipts bind claims to executable evidence.
The failing assertion demonstrates that an incompatible observed component still incorrectly advertises write access.
A recomputed incompatible build identity cannot yet reduce authorization.
Adapter replacement performs no migration or state cleanup.
Adapter exit preserves the same live run and CLI observability.
Registry, runtime state, logs, and spawn receipts survive adapter exit.
Closing MCP never signals the managed process.
Compatible host B observes and reuses host A's run ID and PID without respawn.
Standalone CLI observes logs/state and safely stops the exact surviving run.
